codeReview.summary: The implementation satisfies the feature contract: src/features/baselines.py computes exponent-1.83 Pythagorean win percentage, separate full-game and F5 30/60-game baselines, team-only Log5 matchup probabilities, and persists anti-leakage-safe feature rows. I did not find blocking or non-blocking code issues in the committed implementation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared worker guidance does not document that baseline features can source historical final/F5 runs directly from the SQLite games table, so the worker had to investigate pybaseball team-game-log sources before implementing the feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The transcript skeleton for worker session e43f4fce-5881-49f4-97b9-7dc8238fbbfe shows WebSearch queries for pybaseball team_game_logs columns first five innings plus inspection of C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.venv\Lib\site-packages\pybaseball\team_game_logs.py, while .factory\skills\feature-worker\SKILL.md:19-26 only names Pythagorean/Log5 baselines generically. The final implementation instead uses games.final_* and games.f5_* columns directly in src\features\baselines.py:181-227.

summary: Review passed. The feature meets VAL-FEAT-008/009/010 with correct exponent-1.83 Pythagorean math, separate F5/full-game rolling baselines, team-only complementary Log5 probabilities, and prior-day as_of_timestamp persistence. I only noted a shared-state skill/knowledge gap around documenting the canonical baseline data source.
